---
title: "Publications"
toc: true
toc-title: "Sections"
---

## Published

::: {.pub-entry}
[Extractive versus Generative Language Models for Political Conflict Text Classification]{.pub-title}
[with [P.T. Brandt](https://www.utdallas.edu/~pbrandt/){target="_blank"}, [S. Alsarra](https://www.linkedin.com/in/sultan-alsarra-phd-56977a63/){target="_blank"}, [V. D'Orazio](https://mathanddata.wvu.edu/directory/faculty/vito-d-orazio){target="_blank"}, D. Heintze, [L. Khan](https://www.utdallas.edu/~lkhan/){target="_blank"}, [J. Osorio](https://www.javierosorio.net/){target="_blank"} & M. Sianan]{.pub-authors}
[*Political Analysis*, 2025]{.pub-venue}
[<button class="abstract-toggle" onclick="toggleAbstract(this)">Abstract</button> [Link](https://doi.org/10.1017/pan.2025.10027){target="_blank"} | [Slides](research/ConfliBERT_Presentation.pdf){target="_blank"} | [Code](https://doi.org/10.7910/DVN/KDO5AM){target="_blank"}]{.pub-links}
[We develop specialized language models for political conflict analysis that outperform general-purpose LLMs like Gemma 2, Llama 3.1, and Qwen 2.5 in accuracy, precision, and recall while being hundreds of times faster.]{.abstract-content}
:::

::: {.pub-entry}
[ConflLlama: Domain-Specific Adaptation of Large Language Models for Conflict Event Classification]{.pub-title}
[with [P.T. Brandt](https://www.utdallas.edu/~pbrandt/){target="_blank"}]{.pub-authors}
[*Research & Politics*, 2025]{.pub-venue}
[<button class="abstract-toggle" onclick="toggleAbstract(this)">Abstract</button> [Link](https://doi.org/10.1177/20531680251356282){target="_blank"} | [Slides](research/APSA___ConflLlama.pdf){target="_blank"} | [Code](https://doi.org/10.7910/DVN/0MUGE6){target="_blank"}]{.pub-links}
[We introduce ConflLlama, a specialized variant of Llama 3.1 fine-tuned for political conflict classification, demonstrating superior performance in event coding and conflict analysis tasks compared to traditional approaches.]{.abstract-content}
:::

::: {.pub-entry}
[Public Health Advocacy in Times of Pandemic: An Analysis of the Medicare-For-All Debate on Twitter During COVID-19]{.pub-title}
[with [S. Kumar](https://jgu.edu.in/jsgp/dr-sushant-kumar/){target="_blank"} & [P. Zhang](https://pengfei-zhang.com/){target="_blank"}]{.pub-authors}
[*Behavioral Sciences*, 2025]{.pub-venue}
[<button class="abstract-toggle" onclick="toggleAbstract(this)">Abstract</button> [Link](https://doi.org/10.3390/bs15020223){target="_blank"} | [Code](https://doi.org/10.7910/DVN/OWVD70){target="_blank"}]{.pub-links}
[We analyze how health advocacy groups adapted their Medicare-For-All messaging on Twitter during the COVID-19 pandemic, revealing distinct approaches to public engagement and narrative adaptation.]{.abstract-content}
:::

## Under Review

::: {.pub-entry}
[Strategic Anticipation and Expert Review: The Determinants of Critical Advisory Opinions from the Dutch Council of State]{.pub-title}
[with [C. Egger](https://claramarieegger.wordpress.com/about/){target="_blank"} & [A. Zhelyazkova](https://www.eur.nl/en/people/asya-zhelyazkova){target="_blank"}]{.pub-authors}
[<button class="abstract-toggle" onclick="toggleAbstract(this)">Abstract</button>]{.pub-links}
[What determines whether an expert advisory body issues a critical opinion on a legislative proposal? Using a novel dataset of 2,129 advisory opinions (2004--2025), we find that ideological distance is the most robust predictor of opinion severity, while anticipatory compliance by vulnerable actors reverses expected patterns.]{.abstract-content}
:::

::: {.pub-entry}
[Framework Laws and Fading Oversight: How the Dutch Executive Expanded Its Lawmaking Authority]{.pub-title}
[with [C. Egger](https://claramarieegger.wordpress.com/about/){target="_blank"} & [A. Zhelyazkova](https://www.eur.nl/en/people/asya-zhelyazkova){target="_blank"}]{.pub-authors}
[<button class="abstract-toggle" onclick="toggleAbstract(this)">Abstract</button>]{.pub-links}
[Examining executive predominance in the Netherlands using a legislative dataset spanning 2000--2025. Roughly 75% of legislation now takes the form of secondary executive acts, while MPs retreat toward weaker oversight tools such as motions and parliamentary questions.]{.abstract-content}
:::

::: {.pub-entry}
[Democracy and Internet Control: Theory and Evidence from Transparency Reports]{.pub-title}
[with [P. Zhang](https://pengfei-zhang.com/){target="_blank"}]{.pub-authors}
[<button class="abstract-toggle" onclick="toggleAbstract(this)">Abstract</button>]{.pub-links}
[Examining how democratic and authoritarian regimes differ in their approach to internet content moderation, using data from transparency reports.]{.abstract-content}
:::

::: {.pub-entry}
[Two Types of Censorship? An Assessment of the Informational Autocracy Thesis]{.pub-title}
[with [P. Zhang](https://pengfei-zhang.com/){target="_blank"}]{.pub-authors}
[<button class="abstract-toggle" onclick="toggleAbstract(this)">Abstract</button>]{.pub-links}
[Testing how Guriev & Treisman's Informational Autocracy theory applies to internet filtering practices in autocratic nations.]{.abstract-content}
:::

::: {.pub-entry}
[Build, Borrow, or Just Fine-Tune? A Political Scientist's Guide to Choosing NLP Models]{.pub-title}
[<button class="abstract-toggle" onclick="toggleAbstract(this)">Abstract</button> [Paper](https://www.dropbox.com/scl/fi/sfylpz2bzzhimpkg42zy4/Confli_mBERT.pdf?rlkey=dotkhx1g5eh81uenxrb8eqf6x&st=wz772hjh&dl=0){target="_blank"} | [Code](https://huggingface.co/shreyasmeher/Confli-mBERT){target="_blank"}]{.pub-links}
[A practical decision framework for political scientists choosing among domain-pretrained, fine-tuned, and commercial LLM approaches for text classification. Fine-tuning general-purpose encoders matches domain-pretrained alternatives and outperforms commercial APIs at a fraction of the cost.]{.abstract-content}
:::

## Working Papers

::: {.pub-entry}
[Populism and Investment Treaties]{.pub-title}
[with [C. Peinhardt](https://profiles.utdallas.edu/clint.peinhardt){target="_blank"}]{.pub-authors}
[<button class="abstract-toggle" onclick="toggleAbstract(this)">Abstract</button>]{.pub-links}
[Investigating how nationalist rhetoric influences countries' decisions to exit international investment agreements.]{.abstract-content}
:::

::: {.pub-entry}
[IGO Withdrawal Networks]{.pub-title}
[<button class="abstract-toggle" onclick="toggleAbstract(this)">Abstract</button>]{.pub-links}
[Extending Borzyskowski & Vabulas (2019) to examine network effects in international organization withdrawals.]{.abstract-content}
:::

::: {.pub-entry}
[Media Polarization in International News Coverage]{.pub-title}
[with A. Khalid & K. Park]{.pub-authors}
[<button class="abstract-toggle" onclick="toggleAbstract(this)">Abstract</button>]{.pub-links}
[Using machine learning to analyze ideological patterns in international news coverage across 200,000+ articles.]{.abstract-content}
:::

::: {.pub-entry}
[Event Horizon: Revolutionizing Data Annotation with Reinforcement Learning]{.pub-title}
[<button class="abstract-toggle" onclick="toggleAbstract(this)">Abstract</button> [Model](https://huggingface.co/shreyasmeher/Qwen-GLOCON-Reasoning){target="_blank"}]{.pub-links}
[A novel reinforcement learning framework built on DeepSeek's GRPO, merging political science with advanced AI to create structured, transparent annotations for complex events.]{.abstract-content}
:::

## Dissertation

::: {.pub-entry}
[Digital Sovereignty: The Political Economy of Internet Governance]{.pub-title}
[University of Texas at Dallas, 2025]{.pub-venue}
[[Slides](research/Dissertation_March-31.pdf){target="_blank"}]{.pub-links}
:::

```{=html}
<script>
function toggleAbstract(btn) {
  var content = btn.closest('.pub-entry').querySelector('.abstract-content');
  if (content) {
    if (content.style.display === 'block') {
      content.style.display = 'none';
      btn.textContent = 'Abstract';
    } else {
      content.style.display = 'block';
      btn.textContent = 'Hide';
    }
  }
}
</script>
```
